content: A 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory can be reorganized around a *curiosity gating mechanism* that prioritizes encoding new unexpected experiences using ICM (intrinsic curiosity module) prediction error and RND (random network distillation) embedding surprise as selection signals, instead of the traditional hand-crafted uncertainty-based query selection used in most active learning pipelines. This means the agent’s long-term episodic memory automatically grows by retaining only the high-surprise experiences that most improve its world model, rather than explicitly querying for labeled data on pre-defined uncertain points.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
